subject: [PATCH 1/1] nfsdctl: check for listeners before starting threads

reviews.author: Olga Kornievskaia
reviews.summary: Reviewer Olga Kornievskaia questioned the sufficiency of simply checking if sock->name is non-empty and active, suggesting that additional checks from print_listener() may be necessary for accurate listener counting.
reviews.raw_body:
Well, I thought would need to do all the work of print_listener(), I
would assume we'd want to do all the checks done by print_listener
before it prints the value or in my case count it as a listener. And
thus I opted for re-using the function but adding the count.

If you think it's sufficient to just check if sock->name is non-empty
and active and do no further checking, I can.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about the 'active' flag usage, suggesting to fetch current listeners first and then check if nfsd_socket_count is non-zero
reviews.raw_body:
The "active" flag is only used on the set side of things. Anything read
by "get" is considered active. If you fetch_current_listeners() first,
then I think you can just check that nfsd_socket_count is non-zero.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-03-23.patch_summary: This patch modifies the nfsdctl code to check for existing listeners before starting new threads, potentially improving system performance and preventing unnecessary thread creation.
